summary:
The cemento-dentin junction typically ranges between 2-3 micrometers based on multiple studies indicating similarities in structure and properties compared to other dental structures like enamel-cementum interface where such measurements are well-established at about 2-3 microns as seen through various research references including mechanical strengths observed near those regions which suggest minimal cross-sectional area consistent across different types of interfacial bonds found throughout dentin-pulp complex interactions.
The specific dimensions were supported by several sources noting comparative anatomical features suggesting consistency among related tooth surfaces regarding their microscopic architecture leading us
